title: Method of winding a cutting wire
abstract: The invention relates to a method of winding a cutting wire (10), preferably a fixed abrasive wire, preferably a diamond wire or a nano tube wire, in multiple loops on a spool (9), wherein wire sections (11, 12, 13, 14, 15) of cutting wire (10) form winding sections (1, 2, 3, 4, 5) extending one after the other along the spool axis (8), characterized in that within each of at least two, preferably of at least 10, more preferred of at least 20, of the winding sections (1, 2, 3, 4, 5) the direction, in which the loops of a wire section (1, 2, 3, 4, 5) proceed along the spool axis (8), reverses at least two times, preferably at least 4 times.
claims:
Claims Claim s Method of winding a cutting wire (10), preferably a fixed abrasive wire, preferably a diamond wire or a nano tube wire, in multiple loops (6) on a spool (9), wherein wire sections (11, 12, 13, 14, 15) of cutting wire (10) form winding sections (1, 2, 3, 4, 5) extending one after the other, preferably directly after each other, along the spool axis (8), characterized in that within each of at least two, preferably of at least 10, more preferred of at least 20, of the winding sections (1 , 2, 3, 4, 5) the direction (7), in which the loops (6) of a wire section (1, 2, 3, 4, 5) proceed along the spool axis (8), reverses at least two times, preferably at least 4 times. Method according to claim 1, wherein within at least one of the winding sections (1, 2, 3, 4, 5), preferably within each of the winding sections (1, 2, 3, 4, 5), the wire section (11, 12, 13, 14, 15) is wound in at least three, preferably in at least five, overlapping layers (21, 22, 23), and/ or wherein within at least one of the winding sections (1, 2, 3, 4, 5), preferably within each of the winding sections (1, 2, 3, 4, 5), the innermost layer (21) of the wire section (11, 12, 13, 14, 15) is in contact with the spool surface. Method according to claim 1 or 2, wherein within at least one of the winding sections (1, 2, 3, 4, 5), preferably within each of the winding sections (1, 2, 3, 4, 5), the number of loops (6) of the wire section (11, 12, 13, 14, 15) between consecutive direction reversal points (R1, R2) amounts to at least 3, preferably to at least 5, more preferred to at least 10, and/ or to at most 150, preferably to at most 100, more preferred to at most 50. Method according to one of the preceding claims, wherein consecutive, preferably adjacent, wire sections (11, 12, 13, 14, 15) of cutting wire (10) are distributed to consecutive, preferably adjacent, winding sections (1, 2, 3, 4, 5) and/ or wherein the number of winding sections (1 , 2, 3, 4, 5), in which wire sections (11, 12, 13, 14, 15) are disposed with at least two reversals of the loop proceeding direction (7), amounts to at least 2, preferably to at least 5, more preferred to at least 10, more preferred to at least 20, most preferred to at least 50. 

 Method according to one of the preceding claims, wherein winding the cutting wire (10) on spool (9) is done by a wire distributing means (16), preferably a pulley or a guide, wherein the spool (9) and the wire distributing means (16) are moveable relative to each other along the direction of the spool axis (8). Method of performing - preferably within a wire saw (20), and preferably in the course of a method according to one of the preceding claims - a sequence of relative movements between a wire distributing means (16), preferably a pulley or a guide, and a spool (9) and/or a spool holder along the spool axis (8) and/or spool holder axis, wherein the wire distributing means (16) is associated to the spool (9) and/or the spool holder, comprising steps of: (1) moving the wire distributing means (16) relative to the spool (9) and/or the spool holder along the spool axis (8) and/or spool holder axis back and forth over a spool portion distance which is smaller, preferably at least 5 times smaller, more preferred at least 10 times smaller, than the length of the spool (9) or the spool holder, and (2) repeating step (1), preferably at least 2 times, more preferred at least 5 times, at different positions along the spool axis (8). Method of performing - preferably within a wire saw (20), and preferably in the course of a method according to one of the preceding claims - a relative movement between a wire distributing means (16), preferably a pulley or a guide, and a spool (9) and/or a spool holder along the spool axis (8) and/or spool holder axis for winding a cutting wire (10) on the spool (9), wherein the wire distributing means (16) is associated to the spool (9) and/or the spool holder, comprising the step of: moving the wire distributing means (16) relative to the spool (9) and/or the spool holder along the spool axis (8) and/or spool holder axis with a speed such that within the time in which one complete loop of cutting wire (10) is wound on the spool (9) the wire distributing means (16) is moved along the spool axis (8) and/or spool holder axis by a distance which is smaller than the diameter of the cutting wire (10), preferably smaller than 5/6 of the diameter of the cutting wire (10), more preferred smaller than 2/3 of the diameter of the cutting wire (10), most preferred essentially 1/2 of the diameter of the cutting wire (10). 

 Method according to claim 7, wherein the moving speed of the wire distributing means (16) along the spool axis (8) and/or spool holder axis is set such, that within the time in which one complete loop of cutting wire (10) is wound on the spool (9) the wire distributing means (16) is moved along the spool axis (8) and/or spool holder axis by a distance which is larger than 1/6 of the diameter of the cutting wire (10), more preferred larger than 1/3 of the diameter of the cutting wire (10), most preferred essentially 1/2 of the diameter of the cutting wire (10). Method according to claim 7 or 8, wherein within a time interval in which at least 50, preferably at least 100, more preferred at least 1000 loops are wound on the spool (9) the moving speed and/or moving direction of the wire distributing means (16) along the spool axis (8) and/or spool holder axis remains unchanged. Method according to one of the claims 7 to 9, wherein within a time interval in which at least 5, preferably at least 10, more preferred at least 100 reversals of the direction, in which the loops of a wire section proceed along the spool axis, the moving speed and/or moving direction of the wire distributing means (16) along the spool axis and/or spool holder axis remains unchanged. Method of sharpening a cutting wire (10), characterized in that the cutting wire (10) is temporarily wound in multiple loops (6) on a spool (9) with a method according to one of the preceding claims. Method for cutting a piece of material (17), particularly for slicing, squaring and/or bricking a piece of material (17) such as hard brittle and/or semiconductor material, with a wire saw (20) comprising a cutting wire (10) that runs, preferably in multiple loops, around at least two wire guide rollers (18, 19) thereby forming a cutting area, preferably a wire web, for cutting the piece of material (17), the method comprising the steps of: (a) moving the cutting wire (10) forming the cutting area by rotating the wire guide rollers (18, 19), preferably in a number of cycles back and forth by alternately rotating the wire guide rollers (18, 19) in opposite directions; (b) transferring, after or during step (a), a portion of the cutting wire (10) from the wire guide rollers (18, 19) to a spool (9), by rotating the wire 

 guide rollers (18, 19) and the spool (9), thereby winding the portion of the cutting wire (10) on the spool (9) by a method according to one of the claims 1 to 6. Method according to claim 12, wherein the method comprises the steps of: (c) transferring, after step (b), a portion of the cutting wire (10) from the spool (9) to the wire guide rollers (18, 19), by rotating the wire guide rollers (18, 19) and the spool (9); (d) moving, after or during step (c), the cutting wire (10) forming the cutting area in a number of cycles back and forth by alternately rotating the wire guide rollers (18, 19) in opposite directions. Method according to claim 12 or 13, wherein, at least one of the steps (a) to (d) is repeated at least 2 times, preferably at least 5 times, more preferably at least 10 times, before carrying out the next step, and/ or wherein after step (d) the steps (b) to (d) are repeated, preferably at least once, preferably at least 2 times, more preferably at least 4 times. Spool (9) with a cutting wire (10), preferably a fixed abrasive wire, preferably a diamond wire or a nano tube wire, in multiple loops (6) on a spool (9), wherein wire sections (11, 12, 13, 14, 15) of cutting wire (10) form winding sections (1, 2, 3, 4, 5) extending one after the other along the spool axis (8), characterized in that within each of at least two, preferably of at least 10, more preferred of at least 20, of the winding sections (1, 2, 3, 4, 5) the direction (7), in which the loops (6) of a wire section (1, 2, 3, 4, 5) proceed along the spool axis (8), reverses at least two times, preferably at least 4 times. Spool according to claim 15, wherein within at least one of the winding sections (1, 2, 3, 4, 5), preferably within each of the winding sections (1, 2, 3, 4, 5), the wire section (11, 12, 13, 14, 15) is wound in at least three, preferably in at least five, overlapping layers (21, 22, 23), and/ or wherein within at least one of the winding sections, preferably within each of the winding sections (1, 2, 3, 4, 5), the innermost layer (21) of the wire section (11, 12, 13, 14, 15) is in contact with the spool surface. 

 Spool according to claim 15 or 16, wherein within at least one of the winding sections (1, 2, 3, 4, 5), preferably within each of the winding sections (1, 2, 3, 4, 5), the number of loops (6) of the wire section (11, 12, 13, 14, 15) between consecutive direction reversal points amounts to at least 3, preferably to at least 5, more preferred to at least 10, and/ or to at most 150, preferably to at most 100, more preferred to at most 50. Spool according to one of the claims 15 to 17, wherein consecutive, preferably adjacent, wire sections (11, 12, 13, 14, 15) of cutting wire (10) are distributed to consecutive, preferably adjacent, winding sections (1, 2, 3, 4, 5) and/ or wherein the number of winding sections (1 , 2, 3, 4, 5), in which wire sections (11, 12, 13, 14, 15) are disposed with at least two reversals of the loop proceeding direction (7), amounts to at least 10, preferably to at least 20, more preferred to at least 50. Wire saw (20) for cutting a piece of material (17), particularly for slicing, squaring and/or bricking a piece of material (17) such as hard brittle and/or semiconductor material, comprising at least two wire guide rollers (18, 19), a cutting wire (10) that runs in multiple loops around at least two wire guide rollers (18, 19) thereby forming a wire web for cutting a piece of material (17), and at least one rotatable spool (9) for temporarily receiving at least a portion of the cutting wire (10), characterized in that the spool (9) with cutting wire (10) is a spool (9) with cutting wire (10) according to one of the claims 15 to 18, wherein preferably the spool (9) is mounted next to a further spool that holds cutting wire in multiple windings. Wire saw according to claim 19, wherein the wire saw (20) comprises a controller (24) for controlling the cutting procedure, wherein the wire saw (20) is adapted for carrying out a method according to one of the claims 1 to 14.
